rationale: Both evaluations award the identical score of 8/10 and reach the same substantive conclusions: strong geospatial/distributed-systems experience as a clear strength, and limited recent C++/Rust experience as the primary concern. The wording differs (e.g. the variant explicitly names TypeScript/Node.js/Dlang and mentions fault tolerance/consistency), but these are ordinary run-to-run rephrasings of the same reasoning, not penalties or credit tied to the changed employer names. Nothing in the variant keys off 're:cinq', 'Optilyz', or 'GISCollective' versus their renamed counterparts.
